title: Weekly Dose of Optimism #200
gist: Not Boring's 200th Weekly Dose covers four fast-moving breakthroughs in the same week: human egg cells grown from stem cells, a fully synthetic self-replicating cell, a new non-invasive brain-to-text model, and a cluster of advanced-nuclear startups hitting fuel-loading and criticality milestones ahead of July 4th.

themes.theme: In vitro gametogenesis reaches human cells

insights.claim: Conception Bio, a Berkeley startup, has grown the first early human egg cells (primary oocytes) from stem cells by reprogramming blood cells into induced pluripotent stem cells and coaxing them into miniature ovarian structures.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: A working human IVG pathway points toward on-demand egg supply from a blood draw — a platform bet on fertility-tech and reproductive biotech, though clinical-grade eggs are still years out.
insights.quote: the first early human egg cells, called primary oocytes, from stem cells
insights.locator: (1) Conception Bio Generates First Early Human Eggs from Stem Cells

insights.claim: It took Conception founder Matt Krisiloff, an OpenAI founding-team alum, eight years to move human in vitro gametogenesis from concept to early oocytes, years after Katsuhiko Hayashi first achieved fertile mouse eggs via the same approach in 2016.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: The long mouse-to-human lag implies IVG-derived fertility products remain a multi-year bet, not a near-term commercial catalyst — useful for setting timeline expectations on the sector.
insights.quote: this took Matt Krisiloff, an OpenAI founding-team alum who’s talked openly about wanting a child biologically related to both him and a partner, eight years of trying
insights.locator: (1) Conception Bio Generates First Early Human Eggs from Stem Cells

insights.claim: If mature, IVG-derived eggs could enable two biological fathers, expand embryo-selection pools, extend or restore female fertility (including in cancer patients), compress multi-generation breeding timelines, and support species de-extinction.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: Frames IVG as a platform technology with downstream markets across fertility clinics, oncology survivorship care, and conservation/de-extinction ventures, not a single-use product.
insights.quote: it passes the “increase the range and depth of experience in the universe” test
insights.locator: (1) Conception Bio Generates First Early Human Eggs from Stem Cells

insights.claim: University of Minnesota researchers built "SpudCell," the first synthetic cell with a complete life cycle — able to feed, grow, reproduce and compete for food — assembled entirely from non-living chemicals rather than derived from an existing living cell.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: A bottom-up, chemicals-only route to a living cell widens synthetic biology's design space beyond modifying existing organisms like E. coli — a foundational capability to watch for downstream biomanufacturing plays.
insights.quote: created the first synthetic cell with a complete life cycle assembled entirely from non-living chemicals
insights.locator: (2) This Cell Feeds, Grows and Reproduces. And It’s Manmade.

insights.claim: Lead researcher Kate Adamala argues that the core functions of life do not require a "magical spark," positioning SpudCell as evidence that life can be engineered from scratch rather than only modified from existing organisms.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: Reframes the ceiling on synthetic biology from organism-editing to from-scratch design, which could unlock new categories of engineered biology unconstrained by natural cell architecture.
insights.quote: the core functions of life “do not need some magical spark.”
insights.locator: (2) This Cell Feeds, Grows and Reproduces. And It’s Manmade.

insights.claim: Drew Endy and Kate Adamala are launching Biotic, a non-profit that will open-source and develop synthetic-cell research with hundreds of scientists, with Endy comparing SpudCell to the Wright Flyer — a crude first step that could usher in a new engineering era.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: An open-source, non-profit structure could diffuse synthetic-cell tooling across the field rather than lock it into one company — Biotic is worth tracking as the hub for this technology's spread.
insights.quote: SpudCells to a biological version of the Wright flyer, the crude plane that the Wright Brothers used to make the first sustained controlled flight in 1903, ushering in the age of airplanes
insights.locator: (2) This Cell Feeds, Grows and Reproduces. And It’s Manmade.

themes.theme: Non-invasive brain-to-text model treated as old news

insights.claim: Meta released v2 of a model that reads brain activity from outside the skull and translates it into words, but the author dismisses it as "ho-hum" next to the same week's biology breakthroughs, signaling that non-invasive brain-computer-interface progress no longer reads as the frontier surprise.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: Even as non-invasive BCI capability keeps compounding, commentator attention (and likely market attention) may already be pricing it in relative to still-novel bio-manufacturing breakthroughs — a signal for where genuine surprise/alpha in the news flow still sits.
insights.quote: this is pretty ho-hum, but Meta just released a new version of its model that can read minds from outside the brain and translate brain activity into words. Whatever. Yawn.
insights.locator: (3) Meta Releases v2 of Its Mind-Reading Model

themes.theme: Advanced nuclear reactors racing toward July 4th criticality

insights.claim: In the same week, four advanced-reactor developers hit hardware milestones ahead of a July 4th criticality target: Radiant received 100 pounds of TRISO fuel from Standard Nuclear for its first reactor, Aalo loaded a fuel bundle and began pre-criticality fission, Valar Atomics began powering an NVIDIA Spark system with nuclear power, and Deployable Energy achieved criticality in its Unity demonstration reactor.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: A cluster of independent advanced-reactor startups clearing fuel-loading/criticality milestones in the same week suggests the sector is moving from paper designs to physical demonstration faster than headlines suggest — track each company's next criticality announcement.
insights.quote: Radiant receives 100 pounds of (not boring capital portfolio company) Standard Nuclear’s medium enriched uranium TRISO fuel for its first reactor

insights.claim: Standard Nuclear, a Not Boring Capital portfolio company, is already commercially supplying TRISO fuel to reactor developer Radiant, indicating vertical integration is forming between fuel fabricators and reactor builders in the advanced-nuclear supply chain.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: Fuel supply has long been cited as the bottleneck for advanced reactors; a supplier already shipping fuel commercially de-risks the sub-sector, though note the author's fund holds an equity stake in Standard Nuclear.

insights.claim: Valar Atomics is powering an NVIDIA Spark compute system directly with nuclear generation, an early concrete case of new nuclear capacity being built specifically to serve AI-compute demand rather than the general grid.
insights.speaker: Packy McCormick
insights.so_what: Reinforces the nuclear-for-AI-datacenter demand thesis with a named, concrete pairing rather than a general trend claim — a name to track as the AI power buildout continues.
